unclear where people are getting their conclusions from but i ran the math based off of what we've been given and she's incredibly strong
case 1: OE pollux vs e3/OE mouchette for pure damage, so both are caro teams with thais, titan, and sig (helot for mouchette)
case 2: OE pollux vs e3/OE mouchette in DTide, so this assumes no access to thais, no access to lone seed, no titan, primarily solo DPS without significant team buffs
(these numbers are without vul/any other team buffs like horla, clem, etc)

pros:
- helot exists
- lots of strike support from relics
- quite a few strike supports options from wheels/character
- monochaos
- somewhat weak aliemus generation outside of burst turns
- e3 damage is the exact same as OE, the only reason to roll for OE is the stun utility and 3 arithmetica efficiency

cons: 
- pretty much needs sig wheel
- no defensive layer besides DR so that needs to be made up with from team support
- plays a lot of cards and wants strikes which have no search or retain, so again needs team support
She's strong at e3?
Hm
Like who would you compare her dmg with at e3?
e3 is full power, OE does literally nothing damagewise besides the +1 hit which is w/e since exalt damage, and the -3 cost which saves you some arithmetica (but can be made up for with arith effects)
Also she needs her sig wheel?
i talked with reya about her
she surpasses pollux in power with helot so her comparison is with the top-level meta dps
pollux and gmurphy basically
downside being yet another character that hyperscales with thais/loneseed

She did say that her sig isn't worth it and that lily is better
~~I think~~
And she did say helot is a must
solo damage is strong yet not gamebreaking, she's not doing 6m damage turns like gmurphy but she's also going to have easily enough damage to oneshot every a5 boss in the game with helot

*based off the written description of wheel* (this is a huge disclaimer because we all know how accurate biav wording is), her sig is pretty much mandatory

the cdmg that applies to everything is on the left, the 0% cdmg columns in the middle are card-specific cdmg buffs
basically anything that changes mid-turn or only applies to specific cards/effects

As for the rest of the standard awakeners
- Uvhash needs his OE to be a character
- Leigh's OE is worth considering, though she's quite niche
- Faros' is good, even if not necessary  
- Sanga's OE is amazing, but she needs her own team
- Nautila's is really strong, but she struggles with getting aliemus for it
- Karen's is peak in any mono Chaos team 
- Tinct's is powerful, but not really utilized in the one team she's currently popular in
- Casiah's is good by the virtue of her generating too much aliemus for herself
Aigis and Lotan OE are excellent

Gluttony lets you forsake the orison you get after battle in exchange for +20% max HP, and you can do this twice. This nets you +44% max HP and they also heal you.

Emerge and Frenzy lets you get an extra refresh and do so for free, and let you buy Cursed Relics, which are pretty strong.

keyflare regen every turn based off of total keyflare stat across all chars and how many exalts you DIDN'T use

also, I've heard Blue is dependent on the player level?
ok so the aequor mechanic is tentacles
they deal a certain amount of damage and you can switch them to defense or attack stance
defense stance gives you a shield that scales according to your max hp
and your max hp scales according to your player level
thats probably what people meant when they said that
It scales up to level 63, right?
im not sure
however, it's not terribly important
in fact aequor is best in early game and worst at endgame
this is because at low levels the tentacles hit like trucks compared to your characters, they will solo entire fights for you
but they scale very badly with endgame enemy hp and attack damage
to the point that even with fully stacked account and max keeper level the white stance is a rounding error on the amount of damage you are going to take
so that is what i would say to a new player asking about aequor
got it, thanks
I was curious because currently I'm running Caelus and nautila and basically taking no damage unless I get  unlucky with the cards
stalling is strong early on but the enemy numbers go up faster than your shield numbers
so at some point u are going to need a proactive strategy
that point is going to be like chapter 7 ish

you can still make stall/counter work but its going to require ludicrous amounts of investment compared to just running aurita and tentacle damage or something like that
